title: A grave situation: Medway police seek return of 12-foot-tall skeleton’s head
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Medway Police are investigating the larceny of a 12-foot-tall skeleton's head from the Medway Recreation Center.
The skeleton, named Kevin Skellington, has been part of Choate Park exhibitions since September 2023.
Authorities are reviewing security footage and asking the public for tips to find the missing plastic head.

title: Dropkick Murphys to play at ‘No Kings’ rally on Boston Common
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Dropkick Murphys to perform at 'No Kings' rally on Boston Common on Saturday, March 28 from 2-4 p.m.
The 'No Kings' rally organized by ACLU Massachusetts and others, opposes injustices linked to Trump policies.
The rally is part of nationwide 'No Kings Day' protests, originating during Trump's birthday in June 2025.

title: Sold out show? Not if you book at this Boston hotel.
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Omni Boston Hotel at the Seaport offers an All Access Pass for VIP tickets to sold-out concerts and events in 2026.
The package starts at $1,000 and includes accommodations, transportation, and premium seating for various events.
The hotel provides additional perks like sound check access and VIP lounge entry, with options for suite upgrades.

title: Six from N.H. charged in ‘brazen’ Downtown Crossing assault and robbery
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Six individuals from New Hampshire charged for assaulting and robbing a 19-year-old at Downtown Crossing MBTA station.
The suspects allegedly chased and attacked the victim on an escalator, stealing his phone, ID, and debit cards.
All six were arraigned and released on $500 bail, scheduled to return to court on May 19 for a hearing.

title: Review: ‘Project Hail Mary’ borders on miraculous
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Project Hail Mary' features Ryan Gosling as an amnesiac astronaut on a mission to save Earth from microscopic alien threats.
Co-directed by Phil Lord and Christopher Miller, the film blends sci-fi adventure with buddy comedy, featuring an alien named 'Rocky'.
Despite its entertaining start, the movie's lengthy run and adherence to the novel lead to a faltering conclusion.

title: City Council calls for more transparency from Wu over transportation projects
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Boston City Council urges Mayor Wu for transparency on transportation projects due to new approval policy causing delays.
Council President Breadon raises concerns over $200 million in potential investments threatened by stalled projects.
Councilor Durkan seeks an update on city transportation goals and stresses council's role in street infrastructure oversight.

title: ‘All the negativity that’s in this town sucks’: Revisiting Rick Pitino’s iconic Celtics rant
author: Chad Finn
bullets: Rick Pitino, Celtics coach from 1997 to 2001, delivered an iconic rant during a postgame conference on March 1, 2000.
Pitino's rant, known for the 'Larry Bird is not walking through that door' line, reflected frustration with team performance.
Pitino's Celtics tenure ended with a 102-146 record, overshadowed by the memorable rant and the drafting of Paul Pierce.

title: Joe Mazzulla’s ‘tough love’ reminds Robert Parish of a particular Hall-of-Fame coach
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Robert Parish praises Joe Mazzulla's coaching style, likening him to former Celtics coach Bill Fitch.
Parish admires Mazzulla's intense persona and martial arts background, which he believes influences the team's toughness.
Celtics' success is attributed to their collective teamwork, with Parish highlighting Mazzulla's effective management of player rotations.

title: Lukas Reichel has a tall task sparking dormant part of Bruins’ lineup
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Lukas Reichel will join Boston Bruins' third line against Winnipeg on Thursday, his first game with the team.
Reichel was acquired from Vancouver for a 2026 sixth-round pick and recently scored five points in three AHL games.
Bruins coach Marco Sturm hopes Reichel's speed and skill enhance a slumping third line with Morgan Geekie and Elias Lindholm.

title: A Massachusetts police officer is running the Boston Marathon for veterans
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Laicey Ieronimo, 25, from Carver, Massachusetts, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for Semper Fi & America's Fund.
Ieronimo, a Massachusetts police officer, honors veterans and military members, many being her family, through marathon fundraising.
This marks her third Boston Marathon as a charity runner; she's raised over $40,000 for various charitable causes through running.
